title: Remotes, Refspecs, Pruning, and Safe Publication · History Workshop
summary: A pairing session predicts each repository-state transition before building the first distributed ref mapping and publication safety plan for remotes, refspecs, pruning, and safe publication. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of distributed ref mapping and publication safety plan while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Parse positive and negative fetch and push refspecs, wildcard mappings, force markers, deletion, matching, and configured defaults.
Reason about advertised refs, negotiation, shallow or filtered boundaries, tag following, prune, prune-tags, and remote-tracking updates.
Predict fast-forward acceptance by namespace and object type and distinguish local preflight from server receive policy.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-advanced cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model
steps.instruction:
workshop case — Case G33 is a workshop in a disposable repository. publication is safe only if remote main still equals the reviewed b00b1e5 lease value. The learner must predict the explicit source, destination, and expected old ID expose overwrite authority before mutation, run git push --dry-run --force-with-lease=refs/heads/main:b00b1e5 origin HEAD:refs/heads/main, explain the dry run accepts the exact mapping in simulation and records that no network changed, test this changed condition: advance remote main to c0ffee0 and require the explicit lease to reject publication, and preserve this recovery boundary: retain the old and proposed remote IDs plus a local recovery ref before authorized push.

What must be established before implementation?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for parse positive and negative fetch and push refspecs, wildcard mappings, force markers, deletion, matching, and configured defaults.

content.value: Git Configuration, Hooks, and Credentials Manuals — Controls configuration scope and origin, protected config, safe.directory, hooks, credentials, aliases, and trust.

Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: origin/main is a live alias that automatically follows the remote branch.

options.text: For commit to the mission model, apply Gita Refspec Source Destination without its stated preconditions, omit the distributed ref mapping and publication safety plan trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Gita Refspec Source Destination for distributed ref mapping and publication safety plan.
State the Gita Refspec Source Destination preconditions, preserve earlier contracts, and name the trace or changed-input result that will decide success.
origin/main is a live alias that automatically follows the remote branch. This does not resolve commit to the mission model.

steps.title: Extend the cumulative artifact
steps.instruction: Append an Advanced Git evidence block headed "# Evidence: gita-multi-remote-publication-17gozz" with exact before and after refs; a falsifiable prediction; the bounded simulator command; expected, observed, tree, behavior, changed-case, and recovery evidence; and one honest real-repository transfer limit.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Gita Multi Remote Publication into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: gita-multi-remote-publication-17gozz
# scenario-key: trace-17gozz probe-zzog71 boundary-gozz17 owner-17gozzx fault-17gozzz
# before-ref: main=a11ce17 candidate=b00b1zz
# hypothesis: publication is safe only if remote main still equals the reviewed b00b1e5 lease value
git push --dry-run --force-with-lease=refs/heads/main:b00b1e5 origin HEAD:refs/heads/main
# expected: the explicit source, destination, and expected old ID expose overwrite authority before mutation
# observation: the dry run accepts the exact mapping in simulation and records that no network changed
# changed-case: advance remote main to c0ffee0 and require the explicit lease to reject publication
# after-ref: main=a11ce17 candidate=c0ffeez
# tree-proof: the affected tree and path set match the predicted repository transition
# behavior-proof: a changed input preserves the named product or release invariant
# recovery: retain the old and proposed remote IDs plus a local recovery ref before authorized push
# transfer-limit: a real server may add receive policies and background fetch can change tracking refs
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.text: Case G41 is a workshop in a disposable repository. publication is safe only if remote main still equals the reviewed b00b1e5 lease value. The learner must predict the explicit source, destination, and expected old ID expose overwrite authority before mutation, run git push --dry-run --force-with-lease=refs/heads/main:b00b1e5 origin HEAD:refs/heads/main, explain the dry run accepts the exact mapping in simulation and records that no network changed, test this changed condition: advance remote main to c0ffee0 and require the explicit lease to reject publication, and preserve this recovery boundary: retain the old and proposed remote IDs plus a local recovery ref before authorized push. Competency probe: Capture remote URLs, authentication boundary, exact source and destination, expected old and new IDs, server result, and post-fetch verification.

content.title: Fault-isolation decision
content.text: Select evidence that identifies a cause, not a patch that only hides the Gita Multi Remote Publication symptom in distributed ref mapping and publication safety plan during this workshop case.

options.text: Keep the plausible Gita Multi Remote Publication output and hide the violated distributed ref mapping and publication safety plan assumption instead of explaining the failure. This does not resolve test an acceptance boundary.

For test an acceptance boundary, apply Gita Multi Remote Publication without its stated preconditions, omit the distributed ref mapping and publication safety plan trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Gita Multi Remote Publication for distributed ref mapping and publication safety plan.
Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated Gita Multi Remote Publication condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.
